title: Tire inflation and pressure monitoring tool
abstract: A tire inflation and pressure monitoring tool includes a handheld power tool having a manually-activated switch and a rotationally-driven bit. The switch is selectively operated to drive the bit either clockwise or counterclockwise. The bit is configured to be engageable with and retain a valve stem cover on a tire valve stem. The tool further includes a tire inflator having a first end, a second end, a hollow void extending along the length. The first end and the second end are in fluid communication with one another. The first end has an air chuck for delivering air into a tire via a tire valve stem, while the second end is configured to receive pressurized air from a source of compressed air. The tire inflator further has a pressure gauge for displaying a pressure of air inside the tire inflator.
claims:
Claims 1. A tire inflation and pressure monitoring tool, the tool comprising: a handheld power tool having a manually-activated switch and a rotationally-driven bit, the switch and the bit being cooperatively configured whereby the switch is selectively operated to drive the bit in either clockwise or counterclockwise directions, the bit further being configured to engage with and retain a valve stem cover on a tire valve stem; and a tire inflator having a first end and a second end, the tire inflator including a hollow void extending along the length thereof, and the first end and the second end being in fluid communication with one another, the first end having an air chuck configured to deliver air into a tire via the tire valve stem, and the second end being configured to receive pressurized air from a source of compressed air, the tire inflator further having a pressure gauge configured to display the pressure of air inside the tire inflator. 2. The tool of claim 1, wherein the bit includes a cylindrical inner wall defining a void for frictionally retaining the valve stem cover. 3. The tool of claim 1, wherein the bit comprises a resilientiy deformable elastomeric material to frictionally engage and retain the valve stem cover. 4. The tool of claim 1, wherein the switch is a three-way rocker switch in which the switchâs three positions are associated with (1) driving the bit clockwise, (2) driving the bit counterclockwise, and (2) a stationary position in which the bit does not rotate. 5. The tool of claim 1, wherein the tire inflator includes a manually-operated valve to selectively open or close the fluid communication between the first end and the second end. 6. The tool of claim 3, wherein the switch is a three-way rocker switch in which the switchâs three positions are associated with (1) driving the bit clockwise, (2) driving the bit counterclockwise, and (2) a stationary position in which the bit does not rotate. 7. The tool of claim 6, wherein the tire inflator includes a manually-operated valve to selectively open or close the fluid communication between the first end and the second end. 8. The tool of claim 3, wherein the bit includes a cylindrical inner wall defining a void for frictionally retaining the valve stem cover. 9. The tool of claim 8, wherein the tire inflator includes a manually-operated valve to selectively open or close the fluid communication between the first end and the second end. 10. The tool of claim 8, wherein the switch is a three-way rocker switch in which the switchâs three positions are associated with (1) driving the bit clockwise, (2) driving the bit counterclockwise, and (2) a stationary position in which the bit does not rotate. 11. The tool of claim 10, wherein the tire inflator includes a manually-operated valve to selectively open or close the fluid communication between the first end and the second end. 12. The tool of claim 3, wherein the tire inflator includes a manually-operated valve to selectively open or close the fluid communication between the first end and the second end. 13. The tool of claim 2, wherein the switch is a three-way rocker switch in which the switchâs three positions are associated with (1) driving the bit clockwise, (2) driving the bit counterclockwise, and (2) a stationary position in which the bit does not rotate. 14. The tool of claim 2, wherein the tire inflator includes a manually-operated valve to selectively open or close the fluid communication between the first end and the second end. 15. The tool of claim 4, wherein the tire inflator includes a manually-operated valve to selectively open or close the fluid communication between the first end and the second end. 16. A tire inflation and pressure monitoring tool, the tool comprising: a handheld power tool having a manually-activated switch and a rotationally-driven bit, the switch and the bit being cooperatively configured whereby the switch is selectively 

 operated to drive the bit in either clockwise or counterclockwise directions, the bit further being configured to engage with and retain a valve stem cover on a tire valve stem. 17. The tool of claim 16, wherein the bit includes a cylindrical inner wall defining a void for frictionally retaining the valve stem cover. 18. The tool of claim 16, wherein the bit comprises a resilientiy deformable elastomeric material to frictionally engage and retain the valve stem cover. 19. The tool of claim 16, wherein the switch is a three-way rocker switch in which the switchâs three positions are associated with (1) driving the bit clockwise, (2) driving the bit counterclockwise, and (2) a stationary position in which the bit does not rotate. 20. The tool of claim 16, wherein the tire inflator includes a manually-operated valve to selectively open or close the fluid communication between the first end and the second end.
